extraction.one_line_summary: URAN Rigoberto won a photo finish on Stage 9's mountainous finale after his derailleur broke in the PORTE/MARTIN crash, forcing him to sprint in 53x11 only.
tactical_patterns: ARU attacked Mont du Chat (climb 7) during FROOME mechanical, rejecting traditional etiquette to exploit moment of weakness.
FROOME pre-identified Mont du Chat descent as tactical weapon months before Tour, used descending aggression to pressure rivals into crashes.
Team SUNWEB pushed MATTHEWS over climbs for green jersey points despite mountain finish, revealing non-GC sprint teams' willingness to suffer.
FUGLSANG inexplicably cooperated with yellow jersey FROOME in chase instead of letting him work, tactically naïve for ARU's interests.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Maintained descending aggression even in yellow jersey to time-gap rivals, gained 4 seconds on ARU via technical superiority
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: Crashed at 45mph attempting to match FROOME's descent speed on Mont du Chat, revealed technical limitations under pressure
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: Attacked FROOME mechanical but lacked legs to sustain effort in final sprint, showing aggression without power reserve
rider_signals.URAN Rigoberto: positive: Won despite broken derailleur limiting him to 53x11, showing tactical composure to maintain speed without acceleration option
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Crashed with PORTE at 45mph but remounted to finish 9th, exceptional resilience after high-speed incident
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: negative: Solo attacked final 12km but faded in sprint finish, mistimed effort by going too early on flat run-in
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Lost over 2 minutes to FROOME, significantly worse than expected on stage he should excel
